LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO ENTER JUDGMENT UNDER TERMS OF STIPULATED SETTLEMENT. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. GOOD CAUSE SHOWN AND NO OPPOSITION FILED. JPMORGAN IS ENTITLED TO JUDGMENT IN ITS FAVOR AND AGAINST DEFENDANT APRIL HAMILTON-WEST IN THE AMOUNT OF $35,171.72 AND NO COSTS. Motion To Enter Judgment Under Terms Of Stipulated Settlement
Off Calendar Apr-14-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO ENTER JUDGMENT UNDER TERMS OF STIPULATED SETTLEMENT

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A.'s motion to enter judgment under terms of stipulated settlement is granted. Good cause shown and no opposition filed. JPMorgan is entitled to judgment in its favor and against defendant April Hamilton-West in the amount of $35,171.72 and no costs. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)
